reason: Defines the OMP provider boundary, RPC queue/event mapping, foldable process-block contract, and memory-candidate cleanup requirements for this change.
Required reuse check before adding helpers; existing Shuheng renderer should be reused instead of duplicating UI folding logic.
Required because OMP RPC frames cross provider, queue, renderer, history, and memory-candidate boundaries.
Updated after live OMP smoke exposed that turn_end carries completed text but agent_end is the safe RPC-idle release point for the next prompt.
2026-06-17: Main OMP runtime now uses role main_orchestrator for context packs, initial permissions, runtime task requests, and output contracts instead of inheriting specialist semantics.
2026-06-17: Added regression coverage for main OMP context role, prompt role, full permissions, host tools, and RuntimeTaskRequest wiring.
2026-06-17: Documented that main OMP context packs and runtime task requests must be main_orchestrator/full while subagents remain role-bounded.
2026-06-17: Updated provider documentation to identify the main OMP runtime role as main_orchestrator.
2026-06-18: Opened main OMP full runtime by clearing tools_forbidden and approval_required_for, expanding main_orchestrator tool aliases, and defaulting app-level OMP approval mode to yolo.

2026-06-18: Changed provider-side OMP approval default and fallback RPC command mode from write to yolo; full-profile extension approval fallback now approves mapped tools even for risky-looking prompts when no runtime approval list is present.
2026-06-18: Updated executable OMP permission contract to yolo default, empty runtime deny/approval lists, and full-profile risky prompt approval behavior.
2026-06-18: Documented that isolated OMP defaults to tools.approvalMode=yolo and that the main full context carries no runtime tool deny/approval list.
